name: linear-automation
description: Automate Linear tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): issues, projects, cycles, teams, labels. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Create, search, update, and list Linear issues with proper team and state resolution
Manage Linear projects including creation, updates, and team associations
Work with Linear cycles (sprints) scoped by team with proper ID resolution
Create labels and comments on issues with Markdown support
Execute custom GraphQL queries against Linear API for advanced operations
Resolve entity IDs (teams, states, users) before performing operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 63 static analyzer findings dismissed as false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only (184 lines) describing Rube MCP tool orchestration for Linear API. No executable code, command injection vectors, or dangerous patterns exist. External commands detected were markdown-formatted tool names (e.g., LINEAR_GET_ALL_LINEAR_TEAMS), not shell execution. The hardcoded URL is the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint for user configuration.
valueStatement: Eliminate manual Linear operations by orchestrating MCP tools for issue tracking, project management, and sprint cycles. Focus on work instead of repetitive task management.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server to be configured and connected to Linear
Team-specific operations require valid team ID resolution before execution
Custom GraphQL queries require knowledge of Linear's GraphQL schema
Subject to Linear API rate limits for GraphQL and REST operations

useCases.targetUser: Engineering managers and team leads managing multiple team members
useCases.title: Engineering Team Lead
useCases.description: Automate sprint planning by creating issues from a template, assigning team members, and setting appropriate workflow states without manual Linear navigation.

useCases.title: Product Manager
useCases.description: Bulk create and organize project issues, link them to cycles, and apply consistent labeling across teams for better roadmap visibility.

useCases.targetUser: Developers integrating Linear with their development workflow
useCases.title: Developer Automation
useCases.description: Create issues automatically from code review comments, link commits to issues, and update issue states based on pull request status.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Create a Linear Issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Linear issue with title "Fix login bug" for the Engineering team. Set priority to high and add a description explaining the bug occurs on Safari browsers.

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Search and Update Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all issues in the Engineering team with "bug" in the title that are currently unassigned. Update each one to assign to the team lead and set priority to medium.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Create Project with Multiple Teams
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Linear project called "Q1 Mobile Redesign" spanning the Engineering, Design, and Product teams. Add a description with project goals and create an initial issue for the kickoff meeting.

promptTemplates.title: Expert: Custom GraphQL Query for Cycle Metrics
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a custom GraphQL query to get all completed issues in the current cycle for the Engineering team. Calculate the total story points completed and list issues by assignee.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Resolve team IDs and state IDs using the appropriate lookup tools before creating issues or cycles
Check pagination cursors in responses and iterate through all pages when listing large collections
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode team IDs or state IDs - always resolve them dynamically using lookup tools
Avoid creating issues without first verifying the team exists and the connection is ACTIVE
Do not skip error handling for API rate limits - implement retry logic with exponential backoff

faq.question: How do I set up the Rube MCP connection for Linear?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'linear' and follow the OAuth link to authorize Linear access.

faq.question: Why do I need to resolve team IDs before creating issues?
faq.answer: Linear issues are team-scoped entities. The API requires a valid team_id when creating issues. Use LINEAR_GET_ALL_LINEAR_TEAMS to list teams and find the correct ID.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without a Linear subscription?
faq.answer: No, you need an active Linear account with API access. The skill uses Linear's API through Rube MCP, which requires valid Linear credentials.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination when listing many issues?
faq.answer: Linear API returns paginated results. Check the response for cursor information and pass the cursor to subsequent requests until all pages are retrieved.

faq.question: What priority values should I use when creating issues?
faq.answer: Linear uses integer priority values: 0 (no priority), 1 (urgent), 2 (high), 3 (medium), 4 (low). Always use integers, not string names.

faq.question: Can I execute custom GraphQL queries with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use LINEAR_RUN_QUERY_OR_MUTATION to execute custom GraphQL queries. You need knowledge of Linear's GraphQL schema and should be aware of rate limits.
